content:
3 min read
2 hours ago

--

Everyone keeps asking the same question:
"Which AI is the best?"
After spending months trying different AI tools, I think that's the wrong question.
The better question is:
"How can I make different AI tools work together?"
Instead of relying on just one AI, I started using three free AI tools, each for what it does best.
This is the workflow I use now whenever I want to build an app from scratch.
Step 1: ChatGPT - The Planner
I don't ask ChatGPT to write code first.
Instead, I use it like a product manager.
I explain the idea for my app, what problem it solves, the features I want, and how users should interact with it.
Then I ask ChatGPT to create a detailed prompt that describes the UI/UX as clearly as possible.
The more detailed the prompt is, the better the next step becomes.
At this stage, I'm not thinking about code.
I'm thinking about the user experience.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image from Phone on Chatgpt appsPhoto from my phone on Chatgpt appsStep 2: Gemini — Turning Ideas into a Design
Next, I open Gemini.
Using the free version, I select Gemini Pro and choose Image (Nano Banana).
Then I simply paste the UI/UX prompt generated by ChatGPT.
A few moments later, I get a visual design of my app.
This is one of my favorite parts of the process.
Instead of imagining what the app might look like, I can actually see it.
Sometimes I make a few adjustments and generate another version until I'm happy with the layout.
Now I have a clear blueprint.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Gemini free version appsPress enter or click to view image in full size

UI output from geminiStep 3: DeepSeek — Build the App
Now it's time to write the code.
I open DeepSeek and upload the UI image from Gemini.
Then I use a prompt like this:
«Build an application based on the UI/UX image I uploaded.
Generate complete and production-ready HTML code using vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
Make every feature functional and ensure the application closely matches the design.»
That's it.
DeepSeek generates the complete code based on the design.
Deepseek apps code my promptFinal Step: Deploy It
Once the code is ready, I upload it to GitHub.
After that, I deploy it using Vercel.
Within minutes, I have a live application running on the internet.
No complicated setup.
No expensive software.
Just three free AI tools working together.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Simple functional Apps Using DeepseekWhy This Workflow Makes Sense
I stopped expecting one AI to do everything.
Instead, I let each AI focus on what it does best.
- ChatGPT helps me think and plan.
- Gemini helps me visualize the interface.
- DeepSeek turns the design into working code.
Each tool has its own strengths.
When you combine them, the whole workflow becomes surprisingly efficient.
Final Thoughts
You don't always need premium AI subscriptions to build something useful.
If you're willing to combine the strengths of different free AI tools, you can go from an idea to a working web app in a relatively short time.
This isn't about finding the "best" AI.
It's about building a workflow that works for you.
And for me, this combination has been one of the most productive ways to build apps from scratch.
Need more?
I will share it.
